overall_summary: The American Chemistry Council supports FERC's large-load interconnection rulemaking, emphasizing that chemical manufacturing depends on reliable, competitively priced electricity and that many facilities will need transmission-level interconnection. ACC highlights the sector's role in the semiconductor and AI supply chain and expresses intent to participate in future rulemaking.

quotes.text: Chemical manufacturing relies on highly reliable and competitively priced electricity, and many future facilities will require transmission-level interconnection in order to expand or modernize operations to meet the increasing demands driven by AI, Data Center, and domestic manufacturing.
quotes.concern: Chemical manufacturers need reliable, affordable electricity and transmission-level interconnection for expansion and modernization.

quotes.text: Current practices for connecting large loads to the transmission system vary across regions and often create uncertainty for project timelines and upgrade responsibilities.
quotes.concern: Regional inconsistency in large-load interconnection creates timeline and cost uncertainty.

quotes.text: Chemical manufacturing underpins the semiconductor and artificial intelligence supply chain by providing the critical materials, process chemicals, and infrastructure inputs required for chipmaking and modern data center development.
quotes.concern: Chemical manufacturing is integral to U.S. semiconductor and AI infrastructure, making its electricity access a national competitiveness issue.

quotes.text: Ensuring reliable electricity access for these facilities is essential to maintaining U.S. competitiveness in advanced technologies.
quotes.concern: Reliable power for chemical plants supports broader U.S. technology competitiveness.

bins.name: Consistent interconnection study rules
bins.description: ACC supports clearer, more consistent rules for studying and processing large-load interconnection requests. Current regional variation creates uncertainty around project timelines and upgrade responsibilities, which the rulemaking should address.

bins.name: Chemical sector electricity access
bins.description: ACC argues that chemical manufacturing, as a supplier to the semiconductor and AI industries, requires reliable and competitively priced transmission-level electricity. Securing that access is framed as a national competitiveness priority.

bins.name: Regional process inconsistency
bins.description: ACC notes that large-load interconnection practices vary across regions, creating uncertainty for industrial project timelines and upgrade cost responsibilities.
